title: Discouraged and Humiliated
review: So I ran into some car trouble last week and needed $400 to pay the bill. I tried to transfer the money from my savings to my checking, but kept getting messages saying the transfer was declined and that I needed to contact the bank. Customer service told me it was a large sum and they just needed me to verify it. After that, they said the funds would be released in 2–3 business days. It’s been over a week still no money, still no car. After days of bumming rides and having to Uber back and forth to work, I finally had to break down and ask my mom to cover the bill 🤦🏽‍♂️. This bank is definitely not for the little guy. I’m sure this review will be buried under a flood of positive reviews all of a sudden but at least I can say I tried to warn you. 🤷🏽‍♂️ “May the odds be ever in your favor” IYKYK 😕

title: Great Option for Saving $$
review: This is such a great company for savings accounts and CDs! Their rates are the best and their savings account offers a high interest rate. title: Does not cater to other languages
review:
I’ve been a customer for over a year now. And while I like the app, I’m giving it a low rating because I’m quite unhappy with the company. 

A few weeks ago I attempted to help my mother (who does not speak English fluently) open an account. However, I was disappointed upon hearing that the company does not provide any sort of assistance to customers other than in English, especially in the United States - you’d think that the second most spoken language would be a service that you offer. In addition to that they wouldn’t allow me to act as a translator. 

So, my mom had to take her business elsewhere, we were able to open an account for her with Capital One instead. Their app even has a Spanish interface and Spanish speaking customer service. Do better.

title: Great interest rates but needs better recurring scheduling

title: Devices with Developer Mode is blocked
review:
I’m an app developer who needs to have Developer mode on. This is not the same as jailbreaking or modifying ur device.

Literally every other financial app does not have a problem with this as they know this is no a security issue.

Where as this app force quits. 

It is against the AppStore rules to check if the device has developer mode on as it’s a private API not to be used by 3rd party apps on the Apple AppStore.

title: Terrible customer service, they’ll lock your fund for 2 months
review: Just after 3 months of opening my account my entire fund was held for absolutely no reason for 2 months and I couldn’t pay my rent or make any purchase since all my money is in the bank. I had to send a physical AOI which costs $30 (had to borrow money) and mail it into the office. It took about a week for the mail to arrive and another 2 weeks for the office to review the letters. The customer service was absolutely useless, I’ve made around 20 calls with all the staffs telling me the exact same thing about how they don’t know anything and they’ll have to transfer me. Every transfer to another department is about another 20 minute wait including the identify verification every time. I closed the account instantly after I filed a complaint with the CFPB which they finally unlocked my account. It is by far the worst banking experience I’ve ever had PLEASE NEVER OPEN AN ACCOUNT WITH THEM!!!

title: Do not, under any circumstances, open an account with Marcus…
review:
In March of 2025, I opened an account with Marcus.
After no more than a weeks time, my account was locked, and soon after that, my account was closed completely. They say it’s because I was in violation of one of their policies, and that is their right, and I will not waste time arguing the minutia of what they saw versus what actually happened, but I’m not happy with the fact that they never told me which policy was violated or how I violated the policy. I never got a warning that they were going to close or lock the account. I woke up one morning, logged onto the account, only to find the account was frozen.
As of now, they still have the money that I deposited to open the account in the first place. I’ve placed numerous calls to them, asking/demanding/complaining for my money to be returned to me and as of this writing, I still don’t have my money back. They keep insisting that my money is “perfectly safe with them”. My opinion is that the money isn’t perfectly safe with them because it’s not perfectly safe with me.
To me, this almost seems like theft. They took my money, close the account, and now they’re refusing to give my money back, or at the very least, they’re taking their own sweet time sending the money back. This is mid-May now, so we’re talking about two months or more since the account was closed. That, in my opinion, is a totally unacceptable situation. 
If this can happen to me, it could happen to you as well. Stay away from Marcus at all costs.

title: Don’t wire or your account will be locked.
review:
Thanks for shutting my account down without informing me. I had to call Marcus to figure out why I was locked out of my own account. They could not give me answer (and stated frequently that they did not need to give me answer) and told me my account was going to be closed. Now I have no way to access MY OWN MONEY. If you want to lose access to your funds, with no explanation as to why, then Marcus is for you.
Edit: My account was restored during the following weekday. I’ve already moved all my money out and plan on using another HYSA. I no longer trust Marcus and how they handle issues with their users.
Edit: After reporting Marcus to the FDIC I was given an explanation that it was the employees fault for giving me misinformation about my account and not the company. Unbelievable. Every employee gave me that exact same explanation, but it’s not the companies fault? I’ve stopped doing business with Marcus. At least get your story straight if you want to blame someone else.

title: Marcus has fallen way off
review: A few years ago Marcus used to be one of the best high yield savings accounts out there with some of the most competitive interest rates. Now their rates are not competitive with the other leaders in the industry. Referral bonuses have been cut down as well. And they block linking to competitors to transfer your money out (for example, they won’t let you link to Wealthfront at all). I would no longer recommend this institution when there are better options out there.

title: External account tracking GONE
review: I LOVED being able to view my other banking accounts and credit cards and the categories of my expenses. This used to be my ONLY source of tracking my expenditure categories across all my accounts. And now with the update it’s completely gone. (For banks claiming they want us to budget and know where our money is going… they sure do love taking away the free options for easily doing so.) I know historically this used to be Clarity Money (a free, award-winning app) and Sachs bought it out. But now they’ve just erased all of the tracking functions and charts that made it was it was… and just made it a regular Sachs banking app. Not only have I deleted this app, I will actively discourage anyone from banking here, this is disgusting behavior that actively restricts economic empowerment and public access to financial tools. If you don’t want the costs of supporting an existing public resource, DON’T BUY IT OUT.

title: Couldn’t get my money for a house closing!
review:
I had been a Marcus customer since September 2022 but recently closed my account due to a frustrating experience that left me unable to access my own money when I needed it most. After initiating the transfer for the P&S, I received an alarming call instructing me to contact Marcus. The first agent I spoke with had no idea why I was called, and I was transferred SEVEN times, each time having to re-authenticate with three pieces of information. The agents repeatedly cited ‘quick money rules,’ despite the fact that I had more than five times the necessary amount in my account for over two years, and a significant deposit had been made four months prior.

Eventually, one agent was able to assist, but even she couldn’t assure me that I would be able to wire the funds for my closing. Given this uncertainty, I transferred my entire balance to another bank, which could issue a certified check. This process, however, was equally painful—I spoke with 11 different representatives before finally being able to move my money.

While I valued the high interest rates and ease of use with Marcus, what’s the point of a high-yield savings account if you can’t access your funds when you need them most? This experience ultimately led me to close my account.

title: Locked from Account and no way to Verify
review:
On August 16, I called because my account was locked. They asked for my phone number and told me to wait until August 20. I called again on August 20, and they asked for my phone number and my wife's phone number. They also asked for our driver's licenses, and when they realized the licenses were from California, they couldn’t verify them. I was told to wait 3-4 business days.

When I called on August 23, I was informed that the representatives couldn’t send the one-time password because neither my wife’s nor my phone number was secure enough. They also asked for my license again. I was told that I would need to visit a notary to verify my identity and that I should wait 4-7 business days for the document to be sent.

I called back on August 30, and they started asking for my phone number and license again. I told them that neither was working and that I was calling to confirm that the document was being sent. They ignored me and insisted I provide my phone number again, only to find out it was still not secure. They said I would receive the document to bring to the notary within 7-10 business days from that day. When I spoke to Supervisor Bobby, he told me that the previous 4-7 day estimate was incorrect.

DO NOT USE MARCUS TERRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE! I CASE IS NOT THE ONLY CASE OF INDIVIDUALS GETTING PERMANENTLY LOCKED FROM ACCOUNT.

title: This bank is a literal SCAM
review: TLDR: My experience with Marcus savings has truly been the epitome of shady, incompetent online banking. I'm not someone to write bad reviews, but this has been such a frustrating and ridiculous situation, and I want to warn everyone, please, DO NOT trust this bank. It's one thing if an account is closed and customer service isn't able to provide an explanation behind this, but shouldn't getting the funds back be made an absolute priority? I'm not sure why this bank has deliberately made this become such a complicated and stressful process, where even after 4 YEARS they still have refused to give my money back. I had an account opened with Marcus back in 2020, which suddenly got closed a few months later. When I tried calling customer service, they refused to give me any explanation behind the account's closure. After several attempts to call them regarding getting the money back into my original bank account, I still never received my funds. In July, I had a family emergency that required me to travel for a few weeks - prior to leaving, I desperately tried to call back to see if my money could be sent back to me before I left. They said that a check would be mailed to me within 14 business days and to my surprise, there was no check in the mail once I got back. Again, I tried to call them regarding this, and they stated that their so-called 'review team' would be reopening my account within 30 business days instead. I called again today (which is when I should have received an update regarding the reopening of my account) and yet again, still there is no update regarding if or when I'll be able to get my money back. Endless attempts to speak with both customer service and specialists have been failures, as they are entirely clueless about any updates from the 'review team', and now I haven't been given any timeframe as to when I'll be receiving an update at all. It's rather repulsing to see that a bank with such a good reputation and reviews, being "rated #1 in customer service" can be this negligent and incapable of addressing basic matters.

title: Ok for deposits, bad for GM card
review: I have an online savings account and two CDs with Marcus, and the app is fine for both. My GM credit card was switched from Capital One to Marcus though, and the app is HORRIBLE for credit cards. It lacks even the most basic features like the ability to set up payment reminder alerts. I have never seen such a barebones app for a credit card. I barely use the card anymore because I worry about late payments, maybe one charge every couple months to keep it active after 30 YEARS as an account holder. Sure enough I was a day late on my most recent payment and now have a ridiculous $0.29 finance charge to pay. I will not use the card again and may move my assets elsewhere.

title: Used to be great
review: Marcus has made a lot of changes to the app and its interface in the past couple of months. Taking away the insights aspect of the home page was brutal and I found that it greatly diminished the helpfulness of the app, enough that I considered switching to another bank that offered a more robust app interface. I really didn’t think the app could get worse after they did that. However, one of the more recent updates took away the ability to see the sum of your accounts at the top of the home page. Now if I want to know what I have in total savings across my accounts, I have to do the math myself, and that’s just ridiculous for a banking app. Almost nothing about this app is helpful or user friendly anymore. If you’re new to the app, you probably won’t have many complaints but I doubt that anyone that had the old Marcus app is happy with the current one.

title: Worst Customer Experience I’ve had anywhere
review:
Do your online banking anywhere but here. There are different high yield savings accounts with far better customer service and verification methods. My account has been locked for 6 months and I have no way to access my money or make any transfers or withdrawals. I’ve been on five different 30+ minute phone calls over the course of those months trying to get my account unlocked, but because I don’t live in one of a certain few states, they can’t use anything but my phone number to verify me on my account (not even my SSN, Drivers License Number, or secret word). Even though I put my own phone number on the account, there’s an issue with the Caller ID that the carrier can’t fix so I have no way of accessing my account. 

CHOOSE A DIFFERENT ONLINE BANK! Marcus is not worth the headache.   

If you want your money locked up with no way to access it, you’re in the right place!

title: Scam
review:
I opened an account with help from their customer support. On July 3 they very efficiently accepted the transfer of $5,000 from my bank.

By the next day they unilaterally closed the account because of whatever reason. It is such a kafkaesque process they don’t give you a reason other than they have the right to do so.

They were supposed to return my money in 10 work days. 

Instead use Bank of America (BoA) + Merrill Lynch Edge (ML) or Wealthfront. I’ve been using BoA for 15 years and they are solid. ML is associated with BoA and I find their Edge app better in terms of convenience (instead of sms I can unlock with my face). Wealthfront has also been solid thus far. 

Neither of the competitors mentioned above have done the shenanigans Marcus did.

Avoid Marcus and Goldman like the plague.

title: Why would you axe the only unique qualifier of your product?
review: I cannot believe I am writing a one-star review for the Marcus app. To start, I have enjoyed my experience having a high yield savings account with Marcus. Until recently, you could also link your external banking partner accounts to Marcus to view your overall net worth, transaction history, and spending data. As of May 22, that feature has now been removed, reducing the Marcus app to another failure in a long line of banking apps. Hence this one-star review, for the interface of the Marcus app, not at all to do with the banking products. How disappointing that you had something unique and exciting, and chose to stifle its growth.

title: Used to be Great, not so much anymore
review:
When I first started using this app, it allowed you to check your net worth by connecting all of your accounts to Marcus. That feature was removed and the app became much simpler and not really that insightful.

I still kept my money in there because the Marcus preferred the program gave you an extra 1% APY (5.4% total!) on your savings account for three months per person that you referred. But now as of July 2024, they have completely changed the program and are offering a much less competitive rate/program.

I don’t see a reason to keep my money in here other than maybe to open a CD account.

title: Love Marcus, don’t like the update
review:
I've been using the Marcus app for a while now, and overall, I've been very pleased with it. One of the standout features is the ease with which you can check your accounts. The user interface is intuitive and straightforward, making it simple to navigate through various account details.

However, with the latest update, I’ve encountered an issue that has been quite frustrating. The app no longer provides the previous option where it would show the overall account balance from all accounts at a glance. This feature was incredibly convenient for getting a quick snapshot of my total financial status without having to manually sum up individual account balances.

While I still appreciate many aspects of the app, the removal of this feature has been a significant drawback for me. I hope the developers consider bringing back the overall account balance view in future updates.

title: Not what it used to be
review:
This used to be a phenomenal app until they recently removed a feature called “Marcus insights” (As of 5/22/2024). This feature allowed you to track your expenses from external accounts and provided very helpful tools for managing your finances. With the feature now removed, this app provides no value to your banking experience other than its high interest rate. 

This app literally provides no other features other than to view your savings account and make transfers. I would recommend another banking app with a high interest rate that also provides helpful finance tools.

review:
I love Marcus! They’re one of my favorite financial institutions that is virtual. I don’t have a need to call them often as everything is self explanatory. They literally just updated their app so it’s more streamlined. It’s very fast to log on and access my information. (Some apps take a while to load but not this one). 

However, with the new update, it no longer states the total in all of your accounts at the top. Which I don’t like that. I want to see how much my total savings is. I hope they put it back. Also, my second favorite is with capital one. I wish Marcus would show an accrural amount up to date each month like capital one does. I like watching my interests grow daily until the payout. It’s not a dealbreaker but would be a nice feature.

title: Update ruined what made this app great.
review:
I think I’m speaking for a lot of people when I say that one of the main reasons I use Marcus is because of the app and how beautiful it was to use it. 

The change is too simple. Yes there’s better high-yield rates out there. But I’m not concerned about chasing “the next best thing.” Quite frankly I didn’t have to. The app and the insights were what I really loved and I’ve been happy with it since signing up. 

I understand making things more simple or providing more info with less. This is just too much. The graph was awesome to see how far you’ve come and it motivates me to continue saving more when I can visualize the numbers and not have to just see the number. 

I don’t leave reviews often, but myself and many other will be disappointed & dissatisfied if the graphs and insights aren’t brought back at higher level than it was before.

title: Love the service, upset with one recent change.
review:
I wanna preface that I love the service and recommend Marcus to all my people. 

The recent software update seemed to remove the account growth bar graphs.  I don’t really get much benefit from a more sleek design, especially since the design was already intuitive (at least for my purpose) Honestly, the bar graphs/visual representation was the most beneficial feature to my saving journey. 

One of the things that really motivated me while saving was being able to visualize my account value in the bar graph that the app provided. 
Money has always been tight and it has never been easy to save, so I really appreciated having that visual cue to keep me going. Only looking at numbers doesn’t do anything for me. 

Again, I love this service and the app has always been easy and good to use. This alone isn’t enough to make me switch to a competitor and I still plan on utilizing Marcus I just really hope to see this feature return.

title: Was great until Marcus Insights was removed
review: I wish I could give this app more than 2 stars but when I opened the app today, it was missing the one feature I used it for. That feature was Marcus Insights. This dashboard was a great way to see where I was spending by showing a pie chart of the different categories. I was disappointed to see that it was taken away. I reached out to support to see where it was but was told it was not a feature Marcus offers. I had to get on Reddit to see what was going on with the app. That was one the main reasons I used the app so it’s unfortunate to have to find another app.

title: Terrible security practices
review: Awful security processes that belong in the early 2000s, griefing the user and causing them to ignore warnings. Repeated reauth within a single session, with the same challenge. Switched primary and secondary buttons between cancel and proceed cause you to lose a request. Long 2fa strings, which cause friction without user benefit. Chase does not deserve to be your primary banking service.

title: Reward points reduction
review:
We appreciate the convenience and simplicity of using our Chase Sapphire Reserve card.
We use our card specifically for Travel. It is unfortunate that Chase decided to reduce the points earned for travel related changes from 3 points per dollar spent to 1 point per dollar spent.
While there have been numerous benefits, provided by the updated and more expensive card, we do not use them as they are not benefits, we are interested in.
We would be extremely grateful to Chase if they would go back to 3 points earned per dollar spent on travel expenses.

review: They sell what they can’t offer! I applied for a new card, I had my choice of cards but I decided to stay with chase since I have been with them for a while. review: Chase Business appears to implement significantly stricter security parameters compared to personal accounts. T-Mobile's native IPv6 architecture (utilizing 464XLAT translation) is frequently misidentified by Chase’s security systems as a proxy or an insecure connection. This often triggers an automatic account lockout after just two or three failed handshake attempts, causing major disruptions for business users relying on the T-Mobile network.

I have no idea where this came from so I had to re say my question. They made it a point to not answer my question directly. They actually just said “I see that you have a question about this”. It is so unprofessional and aggravating. Eventually, they were able to tell me that I have to call in and do this on the phone. But it took them about two paragraphs to say this. They could be literally having sex and just doing this on their phone in between ejaculations. They could be playing a video game or driving. There’s no accountability. review: This app is so difficult and tedious to use. Too many layers to everything, and it requires a new screen to set each aspect of a payment—amount, date, and payment account. title: Somehow gets worse
review: Of all the big banks, BOA’s app remains the worst. Where competitors iterate improvements, every new version of this app becomes more crashy, adds more needless steps for simple tasks, and essential features, like transaction disputes, suddenly stop working. Couple this to the bank’s already abysmal offshore customer service, and BOA wins the race to the bottom. I find it hard to believe the positive reviews are not somehow farmed. No actual customer would find this app acceptable.

title: Scam
review: I recently opened a temporary account for a high school reunion. An error in the BOA system caused 2 accounts to be generated. I went back to the branch and they could not close the second account. They said I had to call. Meanwhile charges accrued as there was a “zero” balance on an account I did not generate. When I called they said I have to wait to return the fee and have the account closed. More time goes by and I finally found time to call AGAIN and deal with it. I decide to transfer funds to get back to zero to pay for fees that I never should have had to pay because it was not to my fault that this account was opened in the first place. I talked to 4 people and an hour later. Everyone was “Sorry”, but could not solve the issue. So bottom line. I ended up having to pay for a mistake the Bank of America made in order to not keep having to pay for the mistake that Bank of America made. So sad. And to mention that I have been a customer for 33 years and given them thousands in interest over the years on a line of credit. It’s time to stop doing business with them.  Sincerely. Mr. Dodge

title: What is this??? You hate us?!?!
review:
“You are continuing to a website that Bank of America or Merril doesn’t own or operate. Its owner is solely responsible for the website’s content, offerings and level of security so please refer to the website’s posted privacy policy and terms of use. It’s possible that the information on the website is only available in English. Continue?”

What is this that I have to agree to to continue using the mobile banking app?!?! Why does it feel like I am being given to some hackers?!?!?

I have been banking with Bank of America for decades and they do this sometimes. They used to have a keyboard that was supposed to be secure but it popped into the app weird like it was a virus. I and probably quite a few spoke up about how I thought maybe I had been hacked, but it came after an update and it changed back shortly after. I can deal with a secure keyboard that protects against key-loggers if it is deployed by the institution that is responsible for the storing and moving of my money, but I will not abide the institution that is responsible for storing and moving my money telling me that they have wholly given over the rights and responsibilities of moving my money without ANY information regarding who who the entity is that is now in complete control of my finances so far as I know. 

The way this was presented is a disgusting breach of trust. 

It may be time to finally change banks.

title: UX not that great
review:
For a large corp UX is not that great. Too many taps to archive what one would expect in minimal taps. Accessing Credit card transactions is worse one has to keep tapping each statement instead of simple “last 3/9/12 months” or 2025, 2026 etc options like most other bank apps provides. Merrill app is the worst for trading and again consolidating it here increases the taps taken to reach the sections that trading customers might frequent. No dark theme option to reduce glare and strain on eyes from white background pages, as well as drain battery life from white screens. If an app is not easy on eyes (especially number of users who would be certain aging category) it’s not a good one.
Merrill Notifications at times keep showing up even after accessing them (again the taps taken to access as well as inability to directly access the notification info after tapping from iPhone notification message makes Merrill notifications experience bit of worst). 
Upcoming devaluation preferred rewards tiers from 75% to 50% is surely going to reduce the usage (nothing to do with UX or app just the upcoming unpopular policy going to alienate lot of, guess majority %, customers in preferred honors category)

title: HATE the new credit card payment process …
review:
Went to make a payment on husband’s card, and found that the linked bank account we make payments from had been deleted, not by either of us, we aren’t sure how or why (we have been making payments from that account for years and even made a payment from that account a couple days before without issues). Re-linked the same exact bank account, but now the payment process is totally different. Now we have to select which bank to pay from (there’s only one), then select between the statement balance or custom payment, and select the date to make the payment on. These are all on separate pages with too many button clicks. After that, the screen goes immediately to the payment activity page instead of back to the main credit card page I went into the payment page from that shows all transactions on the card and the balance. 

When I go into my card to make a payment, everything is on one page, with the normal bank account defaulted as the selected option, and the statement balance or enter custom amount option available to choose from, and the current date selected as the default already, with a link to change if desired (which I never change). This process is WAY smoother and preferred across all credit cards as the payment process. 

I do like that when the payments are entered, the pending payment and updated balance after the payment will be made shows immediately on the overall credit card transaction page, but wish that was the only change instead of the overall payment process. The new process has WAY too many button clicks and is very time consuming and not ideal for the end user in the slightest.

title: The UX team must hate users
review:
How does this app have a 4.9 rating? Must be the same group of Wells Fargo employees giving themselves five stars in the break room.

It lacks basic features that every other bank, and every fintech company launched last Tuesday, has had for years. Transfers feel clunky, bill pay is buried like it’s hiding from the user, and good luck getting anything useful out of Fargo “AI”. 

It’s clear the product and UX teams don’t listen to user feedback at all. Almost like the app was designed to please one single 72-year-old executive who still thinks “intuitive” means “exactly like the 2009 desktop site, but on your phone.”

The app is well made and easy to use. The problem is the bank behind. Example:

Wire transfer at Bank of America, AMEX, U.S. Bank, PenFed, Citi, CHASE, etc. $25-$30 and takes minutes to a couple hours.

Wire transfer at Wells Fargo $25 and takes couple days.

It is great for keeping money that you don’t need to move. Also great for slow-pace, pleasant experiences at the counter (geared towards elderly customers). If you need speeds, standard for the current decade, go somewhere else.

title: Peaceful & Impressive
review:
The ease of WF app is extremely calming and impressive.  Face ID opens the app for me. 
Within seconds I can see all my accounts with the most up to date transactions. Having my bills paid through automatic payment keeps me worrying free. 
The notifications estimate future payments in case I forget about a quarterly or yearly payment.  
I like my credit rating shown & updated monthly. Also seeing the deals available for 2-10% cash back with my Visa cash card. 
Lastly I thank WF greatly for being able to call if necessary, without having to have AI try and help me when I want to speak to a human. Your app is fantastic in my book and gives me peace of mind.

title: Not worth the hassle.
review: Money hungry fkrs. 15 fee on this account, 15 on that one, oh but here’s 4 cent in interest to make you feel better about being fkd over lol. Just go to Capital One. They aren’t much better on interest rates (60 cent is definitely better than 4 cent though) and they don’t have made up fees for holding your money. Wells Fargo has become bottom of the barrel banking and they’re desperate for a buck. I’ve been sending all of my money through Zelle to my Capital One account just so Wells Fargo can’t touch it anymore. Greed is bad for business. And don’t even think about getting your money back through a dispute, they won’t honor it. They’ll wait 3 months and then recharge you for kicks. Once all of my accounts and direct deposits are switched over, I’ll be leaving a single dollar in the account. And right before their fees come back around, I’ll be closing the account. title: Locked me out of my account
review:
I opened a new account, funded it, received a direct deposit, and attempted to send money with Zelle. Rather than just denying my transaction, it locked me out of my account, so now I can’t even see my money, much less use it.

I called their support line, which had rather poor quality phone technology which made it hard to understand the person on the other end, and verified my identity in multiple ways as they specified. After all of this, the individual told me that it didn’t matter that they had verified my identity because I still wouldn’t be able to access my account until I receive my card, why could be in over a week.

It is bewildering to me why you would not make this known to a customer. Why would you allow a customer to set up Zelle, verify their identity, and then be locked out of their account with no way to access it until they receive their debit card, assuming no delivery complications? This is a horrible and unfair policy. Why would you not simply prohibit Zelle set up until they receive their debit card?

I am extremely disappointed with this app and probably will not bank with Citi for much longer after this experience.

title: Unreliable, not user friendly
review:
The Citi Mobile app is one of the most frustrating banking experiences I’ve had. The interface is unintuitive and error-prone in ways that have real financial consequences.
I initiated a transfer FROM my account, and the app processed it as a transfer TO my account, the exact opposite of what I commanded. This wasn’t a minor inconvenience. The mistake overdrafted my other account, causing fees and financial stress that should never result from a simple transfer.
When a banking app can’t correctly process the direction of a fund transfer, it isn’t just a UX problem. It’s a liability. I expect basic reliability from a financial institution’s app. Citi Mobile failed that standard completely. I’ll be managing my transactions through a branch or a more dependable platform until this is fixed.

So user beware. There is nothing on the app to alert you the card is frozen.

title: Predatory
review: This is probably the most predatory credit company app I’ve used. The way they post your total is confusing. Payments post immediately but aren’t always reflected in the pending balance. The posted totals never match your balance. Most companies will have a way of showing the running total in your transactions but Citi does not, so your posted total will always lag behind by over a week, leading to confusion about whether the balance matches your spending or not. Payments will post to your pending balance showing negative and then your actual balance won’t match that. Other companies I’ve used will compensate for this posted total vs running total discrepancy but Citi does not, so good luck knowing how much is due. I’ve paid too much several times to keep up with the balance, paid too little others. The only way I can compensate is by keeping a separate log. In which case what is the actual point of using this to help track spending? I am shopping for a new credit card and plan on lodging a complaint with Cosco to make sure they’re aware how bad this company is. A shame that Cosco is associated with this garbage. Hopefully they’ll pick a new company with better spending tracking so that I can use their card again.

title: Lied about APR
review:
I’m very disappointed with my experience applying for this credit card. When I first applied online, the offer clearly stated that I would receive 0% APR for the first 2 years. However, after receiving my card and reviewing the official terms, I discovered that the promotional APR is only for 1 year.

Additionally, the penalty fees and annual fee listed in the final agreement were different from what was originally presented during the application process. This lack of transparency is frustrating and misleading.

I would not have applied if the correct terms had been clearly disclosed upfront. I strongly encourage others to carefully review all final documentation before accepting any credit card offer.

I called right away and closed my account

title: Not user friendly
review:
Suggestions to app developers: please make it easier to find info. For  example, users should be able to see on their main credit card page when a credit card payment is due and whether we have a payment scheduled. Currently you have to navigate to a payment history page and then select a card to find that info…too many steps. For comparison, Chase Bank app has even added this info to users’ dashboard (a green checkmark when no payment is due, or when payment is scheduled). 

Also please add the ability to enroll in special card offers to the app. For example, I want to enroll in the Citi Dividend quarterly 5% bonus category in the app. Currently users have to login to the Citi web site to do this. Again, the Chase app is much more useful and allows enrollment in Chase Freedom quarterly offer in the app.

Citi cards have some good features but the app makes it very difficult to access. Sometimes feels like info is hidden from the user, whether intentional or just poor design.

title: Extremely Frustrating and Unprofessional Customer Service
review:
I recently received a replacement Citi Costco Visa card. After activating it, I tried to add it to my Apple Pay — but it simply wouldn’t go through. I contacted Citi customer service three separate times, speaking with three different representatives, and none of them were able to resolve the issue.

Each call dragged on with long hold times and unnecessary “security checks,” even after I provided all requested information multiple times. Despite more than an hour combined on the phone, the problem is still unresolved. What’s most frustrating is that I never had this issue with my previous card, so something clearly changed on Citi’s end.

As a loyal Costco member for nearly 40 years with an excellent 850 credit score, I expected a much higher level of professionalism and technical competence. Instead, I experienced confusion, lack of training, and poor communication from every representative I spoke with.

Citibank needs to seriously review and improve its customer service training. Representatives should be knowledgeable, courteous, and able to handle basic issues promptly. This was one of the most disappointing customer service experiences I’ve ever had — enough to make me reconsider using this card altogether.

title: Find another credit card company
review:
I’ve had a really disappointing experience. I never received any alerts about a payment issue until two days after it failed — and it was barely over $100. Then, three days later, I find out the payment was returned with no notice and I was charged both a return fee and an interest fee.

I check my cards daily, and there were no app notifications or emails until five days past the due date saying my payment was late. That’s unacceptable. To make things right, I paid off the full balance immediately — over $1,300 — yet when I contacted support to request a courtesy waiver for the fees, I was told they couldn’t do anything.

That’s too bad, because I have a $9,700 purchase coming up this week, and it’ll be going on my other daily-use credit card (you know, the one from A–Z). They give the same 2% cashback and better service.

This card is only really good for gas cash back at Costco, but with all the options out there now, even that’s not enough to justify the hassle. Chase treats customers better.

title: Frequent Forced Unnecessary Updates!
review:
It seems that about once a month, I am forced to update for “bug fixes and performance improvements”. 

If the app is working fine for me, why must I update it? Updates typically cause the app to use more flash memory every time, and my phone only has 64GB, so I don’t appreciate the bloating! 

The only valid reason for forcing updates is to fix security flaws; otherwise, “If it ain’t broke, DON’T FIX IT!”

title: Terrible banking app
review:
This app is absolutely terrible. My card was locked after making a simple Zelle transfer to my own account. I called support, and after more than an hour of back and forth, they finally unlocked it in the morning. But by the afternoon, it locked again—this time claiming there were two “suspicious” Zelle transactions. My account was frozen once more.

To make matters worse, the app asked me for my full bank account number, which I could only get by either logging into the app (which was locked!) or calling support again. I ended up stuck on the phone for over an hour, repeating my issue to multiple agents, just to retrieve my own account information and unlock access.

What a complete mess of IT systems and customer service. It’s frustrating, time-wasting, and makes basic banking painful. Citi really needs to fix this broken process.

review: Citibank is absolutely unsatisfactory. We opened a card and did a balance transfer under the impression that it was zero interest. After another call, we discovered that only a portion of the payment was applied to the current amount, with the rest going to the balance transfer. We paid the entire balance in August, but lo and behold, we were charged interest yet again. This company is so terrible and deceitful; I would never recommend using them as they are absolutely unsatisfactory.

title: Virtual Numbers Not Working
review:
I gave them more than enough time to fix virtual account numbers in the app but at this point it’s just ridiculous.  

Anytime I try to create one or modify a current one I get a big red error at the top saying they’re experiencing issues.  

Also (not app related) but the new rendition of virtual account numbers from website are getting to be less and less useful.  “Daily” limits are now set amounts in large increments as if you’re gonna always know where your electric bill will fall?  So you need to make the limit way higher than it needs to be.  And you can no longer edit the limit.   I liked to put mine at $1 when not using it and raise it to the (exact) amount only when needed.  

So a less useful service that is totally useless from a mobile device, the thing most use more often these days. 

Do better folks.

title: Virtual card number broke since Aug 24
review:
Citi updated their virtual card number functionality on Aug 24 (requires new numbers for those saved with a merchant, no longer can specify an expiration date (it’s now fixed at three years), can only limit dollars to four values via a pull down, or unlimited — I dislike the new limitations on date and dollars).

Generating a virtual number on a their web page works, but  the app won’t generate virtual numbers since August 24, and sometimes won’t let you go into Virtual any all, so you can’t see existing virtual numbers or transactions. 

It’s also harder to get to virtual numbers. 

Prior to this I would have given the app five stars.

title: Citi VAN changes are a disaster
review: The virtual account number feature needs to be reverted to it's previous state. The recent changes have added so much red tape and inconvenience as to make it "virtually" unusable. Bring back the old interface in the app, eliminate the need to log in and generate a new CVV for each transaction, and bring back flexible, changeable limits that can't be overridden by merchants! The new VAN screen in the app only displays the numbers in a small lower section of the screen. Terrible UX!

Finally, a credit card app that’s for 4+ because nothing screams “financial responsibility” like a child who still eats glue managing revolving credit.

title: Virtual accounts is why I got this card...
review:
...So why make it nonfunctional?
Supposed "improvements" to Virtual accounts were made over the weekend.
Now, you cannot generate a virtual number on the app nor on the desktop site.
Desktop site has even more limited functionality than app. You cannot choose an expiration date (they give you "three years" for the credit card)
You cannot choose dollar limit, only a daily allowance maximum but you can only choose 1 of 5 preset amounts, with maximum of $300.

The Citibank Virtual account program has now become worthless.
Been with them for years, but now I guess it is time to move on to a different credit card altogether.
Very disappointed in the "improvement to virtual accounts".

title: Extremely poor customer service
review: Called customer service to activate my mother’s card because she doesn’t speak English and I had the phone on speaker. The representative, Connie (or something like that), asked to speak with the account holder, my mother, which is standard for security purposes and I understand. I told the representative that my mother did not speak English very well, but she insisted on speaking with her. After asking the general questions (Name, last 4 of social security, address), Connie asked my mother what her security word was, which my mother didn’t know and didn’t understand what it meant. So, my mother said she didn’t know, which Connie didn’t hear/understand, so I explained that my mother didn’t understand and didn’t know what that was, and Connie rudely interrupted me stating she could only speak to the account holder and no one else. This obviously upset me, as I was just trying to help and my mother even apologized in her broken English for not understanding and stated that I was her daughter, trying to convey that it was alright to speak to me, for me to translate for her, which is what I was already there for. In total, it took over 20 minutes for Connie to get a translator, confirm all the security answers and my mother’s identity, and activate the card because Connie did not want me to translate and the interpreter got some information wrong. The card could have been activated in 5 minutes give or take, if she had just let me translate, but noooo. And chat support is slow as well, taking 20 minutes to respond.
